title: notifications-in-app — model relationships
description: This module is a THIN transport binding on top of `notifications`. It contributes an in-app-channel driver + a small `InAppReadReceipt` companion for tracking read state. All notification content, categories, templates, deliveries, and preferences live on the parent `notifications` module — this module OWNS no primary domain entity.

outbound.description: One read-receipt per (delivery, user). Tracks first_seen_at + read_at + dismissed_at for in-app bell UX. Optional — the base `notifications::NotificationDelivery.read_at` covers the simple case; this side-table is only added if per-user multi-recipient in-app delivery diverges from the base model.

inbound.description: The base `notifications` module fans out to this transport by looking up the `in_app` channel driver via the ChannelResolver binding this module registers. NO inbound FKs — the driver contract is behavioural, not schematic.

consumed.usage: The transport driver reads Notification.data + resolves the in-app payload.
Read state (read_at, dismissed_at) written back on the base row via the transport driver.
Per-category channel enablement respected (in_app on/off per category).

notes: The module is intentionally minimal because in-app delivery does not have subprocessors, DPA obligations, or complex retry semantics (unlike mail/push/sms). It writes to the same tables as base notifications, over WebSockets/broadcasts.
See parent module for full relationship graph: `notifications/relations.json`.
